excerpt: Qurʾān and Furqān treated as twin names of a single revelation: the gathering speech (qurʾān) and the discriminating speech (furqān), corresponding to the unifying and distinguishing dimensions of prophetic teaching.
source_authority: Qadi al-Nuʿman ibn Muhammad al-Tamimi (d. 363/974), chief jurist of the Fatimid daʿwa and foundational authority on Ismaili taʾwīl
source_location_hint: Discussion of the Qurʾān–Furqān pairing as twin names of one revelation

excerpt: The Pen (al-Qalam) of the Qurʾānic oath is identified with al-ʿAql al-Awwal, the first emanation, through which all subsequent realities are inscribed upon the cosmic order.
source_authority: Hamid al-Din al-Kirmani (d. ca. 411/1020), Fatimid daʿī and chief theologian under al-Hakim bi-Amr Allah
source_location_hint: On the identification of the Pen with the First Intellect within the cosmic hierarchy

excerpt: Relation of the Pen and the Preserved Tablet within the hierarchy of emanation, by which the meanings of revelation pass from the First Intellect into the receptive soul before issuing in prophetic speech.
source_authority: Nasir-i Khusraw (d. after 465/1072), Fatimid daʿī of Khurasan, philosopher and poet
source_location_hint: On the cosmic hierarchy linking Pen and Tablet to prophetic revelation
